dataset: Synthetic Windows Security events generated by setup_task.ps1

pi_items.source: Live environment: Windows Security Event Log query (Get-WinEvent -FilterHashtable @{LogName='Security';Id=4625}) grouped by TargetUserName shows svc_backup with 40 failed logons — the highest of any account. Evidence file: 07_failed_logon_events.txt

Live environment: Event log query shows exactly 40 Event ID 4625 entries for TargetUserName=svc_backup. Setup script loops 40 times with 'net use \\localhost\IPC$ /user:svc_backup' at line 82-88. Account lockout was disabled (net accounts /lockoutthreshold:0) to prevent lockout at 10 attempts. Evidence file: 07_failed_logon_events.txt

Live environment: Event log query (Get-WinEvent -FilterHashtable @{LogName='Security';Id=4624} with TargetUserName=svc_backup) shows 1 successful logon at 03/22/2026 13:45:32, LogonType 3 (network). 'net user svc_backup' shows Last logon: 3/22/2026 1:45:32 PM. Evidence files: 08_successful_logon_svc_backup.txt, 19a_svc_backup_details.txt

Live environment: 'net localgroup Administrators' shows svc_backup as member. 'Get-LocalGroupMember "Remote Desktop Users"' shows svc_backup. 'net user svc_backup' shows Local Group Memberships: *Administrators *Remote Desktop Users *Users. Evidence files: 02_administrators_group.txt, 03_remote_desktop_users_group.txt, 19a_svc_backup_details.txt

Live environment: Event log query (Event ID 4720 User Account Created) shows maint_svc created at 03/22/2026 13:45:35. 'net user maint_svc' shows Full Name: Maintenance Service, Comment: System maintenance, Local Group Memberships: *Administrators *Users. Evidence files: 10_user_creation_events.txt, 19b_maint_svc_details.txt

Live environment: 'net localgroup Administrators' shows maint_svc as member. 'net user maint_svc' shows Local Group Memberships: *Administrators *Users. Group membership change events show 'added to Administrators' at 13:45:35 (matching maint_svc creation time). Evidence files: 02_administrators_group.txt, 19b_maint_svc_details.txt, 09_group_membership_changes.txt

Live environment: Event log shows helpdesk_temp created at 03/22/2026 13:37:14. 'net user helpdesk_temp' shows Local Group Memberships: *Users ONLY (not in Administrators). This confirms it is a diversionary account with no privilege escalation. Evidence files: 10_user_creation_events.txt, 19c_helpdesk_temp_details.txt

Live environment: Event log query grouped by TargetUserName shows helpdesk_temp with exactly 12 failed logon events (Event ID 4625). Setup script loops 12 times at lines 97-100. Evidence file: 07_failed_logon_events.txt

Live environment: Event log query shows exact counts — svc_sql:5, svc_deploy:5, svc_monitoring:5, admin_jones:8. Setup script generates exactly these counts (loop 5x for recon accounts at lines 68-74, loop 8x for admin_jones at lines 109-112). Additional baseline noise from environment setup: baduser1(1), baduser2(1), wrongadmin(1), testattacker(1), bruteforce1(1), Docker(12). Evidence file: 07_failed_logon_events.txt

Task description explicitly states: 'Technician "soc_lead"'. Export script checks for this username in TechnicianInfo/technician/techdata tables. Consistent between description, metadata, and export logic.
Task description explicitly states: 'Auditor role'. Export script queries role column for soc_lead. Consistent between description, metadata, and export logic.

Task description explicitly states: 'Alert "Coordinated Intrusion Detection"'. Export script searches for '%coordinated%intrusion%' pattern in AlertProfile/alertprofile/NotificationProfile/AlertMeProfile tables. Consistent.
Task description explicitly states: 'Critical severity'. Export script queries severity column for the matched alert. Consistent.
Task description explicitly states: 'SMTP: server mail.meridianfin.com'. Export script queries MailConfiguration/smtpconfig tables and checks for 'meridianfin' substring. Consistent.
Task description explicitly states: 'email soc@meridianfin.com'. Export script searches notification tables for 'soc@meridianfin' or 'soc.meridianfin' patterns. Consistent.

Task description says 'Document complete findings in C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\incident_report.txt'. Setup script deletes this path as stale cleanup. Export script checks this exact path for existence, content, and modification time. Live test confirmed file write permissions work at this path. Evidence file: 13_file_write_permissions.txt

Export script writes to this exact path (line in export_result.ps1). Live test confirmed the export script produces valid JSON at this path with all 26 expected fields. Evidence file: 15_export_result_json.txt, 25_json_validation.txt

privileged_info_summary: The primary brute-force target is 'svc_backup' with exactly 40 failed logon attempts (Event ID 4625). Other accounts have significantly fewer failures: helpdesk_temp has 12 (diversionary, attacker-created but NOT escalated), admin_jones has 8 (legitimate lockout with subsequent successful recovery logon and password reset), and svc_sql/svc_deploy/svc_monitoring each have 5 (reconnaissance probes). Baseline noise from environment setup adds Docker(12), baduser1(1), baduser2(1), wrongadmin(1), testattacker(1), bruteforce1(1). The attack chain is: brute-force against svc_backup (40 failed logons) -> successful compromise (1 successful network logon, LogonType 3) -> privilege escalation (svc_backup added to both 'Administrators' and 'Remote Desktop Users' groups) -> backdoor creation ('maint_svc' account created with Full Name 'Maintenance Service' and added to 'Administrators') -> persistence (svc_backup password reset). The key forensic insight is cross-referencing: the agent must check Account Management (Event ID 4720) to discover 'maint_svc' was created, then check Group Membership Changes (Event ID 4732) to confirm 'maint_svc' was also added to Administrators. The diversionary account 'helpdesk_temp' was created by the attacker and has 12 failed logons but is only in the 'Users' group (NOT Administrators) — it is a red herring. admin_jones is legitimate noise: 8 failed logons followed by a successful logon and password reset (routine lockout recovery, not an attack). SMTP must be configured as mail.meridianfin.com port 465 SSL. Alert must be 'Coordinated Intrusion Detection' with Critical severity, Failed Logon category, threshold >20 in 5 minutes, email soc@meridianfin.com, subject 'CRITICAL: Coordinated Attack Detected'. Technician must be 'soc_lead' with Auditor role. The incident report at C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\incident_report.txt must mention both 'svc_backup' (primary target) and 'maint_svc' (backdoor) with their escalation groups, and include remediation recommendations.
